No crypto Package, No Node.js — The Web Crypto API Does It All in the Browser

Sooner or later you'll need to hash, encrypt, or decrypt data in the browser. In the past that meant Node.js's crypto module or a third-party package like crypto-js. But modern browsers already ship the Web Crypto API, so you can do all of it with zero dependencies. This post walks through hashing, AES encryption/decryption, HMAC signing, RSA, and a few handy helpers.
jQuery Was Once King — So Why Did It Fall Off?

A while back someone asked me why the industry mostly recommends Vue, React, and Angular these days instead of jQuery — even though they still use jQuery at work every day. They were curious what's actually behind that shift. So I wrote this post to talk through it: how jQuery rose to the top, and why it eventually got left behind.
What Are JavaScript Generator Functions (function*)? The Function You Can Pause and Resume

function* is one of those syntaxes hardly anyone reaches for, but it's surprisingly powerful. It lets you define a special kind of function called a "generator function" — one you can pause and resume at will. In this post I'll walk through what generator functions are, how yield works, and the real situations where they actually come in handy.
JavaScript Number Formatting: The Easiest Way (Thousands Separators and Currency)

How do you quickly format a number into a thousands separator or currency format in JavaScript? This post rounds up three approaches — toLocaleString, regular expressions, and Intl.NumberFormat — and compares the old-school way with the easiest modern way to get it done.
5 Ways to Pull Values Out of a JavaScript Object

Objects are one of the data types you touch the most in JavaScript, so it's worth knowing all the ways to grab values out of them. This post walks through five: dot notation, bracket notation, destructuring, optional chaining, and the Object.keys/values/entries trio. A quick reference you can come back to.
Vue vs React: What's Actually Different, and How to Pick One (Plus Common Interview Questions)

What's actually different between Vue and React? This post starts from where each framework came from and what it was designed for, covers the framework-vs-library distinction, then compares ecosystem, learning curve and debugging to explain why React ended up more popular — handy for interviews too.
A Few Filter Tricks: Drop All the Falsy Values in One Line

We reach for JavaScript's array `filter` method all the time to pull out the elements we care about — say, every number bigger than 10. It's one of the most-used array methods out there. In this post I'll show a few small tricks for filtering out falsy values, plus how to do the same thing when you're working with arrays of objects.
Generate a UUID Right in the Browser — No npm Package Needed

When you're writing a blog post or a quick demo, you almost always end up faking some data — and that fake data usually needs a unique value, the classic key/id. This post walks through how to generate a unique key/id without pulling in any package at all, using the browser's built-in UUID support.
A Friendly Look at Functional Programming in JavaScript: Currying

Functional Programming has this one really neat idea called Currying. In this post I'll walk through what Currying actually is, how to build it from scratch, and where you might use it in real code. Spoiler: it's not some fancy new syntax — it's just closures doing their thing.
Prettier Tutorial: You'll End Up Using It Anyway — Why Not Start Now? (Full VSCode Setup)

Prettier is one of those frontend tools you have to know. Even if you're not using it today, you'll end up using it eventually — so why not start now? This post walks through installing Prettier, configuring it, setting it up in VSCode, and how it differs from ESLint.
